title: Strike Timeline & Latest Updates
description: Comprehensive timeline of the 8-month Air Canada flight attendant labor dispute, from contract expiry through government intervention and ongoing developments.

sectionsDescription.events: Complete chronological sequence of events from contract expiry through current developments
sectionsDescription.updates: Current information about arbitration process, operational recovery, and ongoing issues

contractExpiry.title: Contract Expires
contractExpiry.description: 10-year collective agreement ends. Flight attendants seek improved pay, ground time compensation, and parity with Air Transat staff.

negotiationsBegin.title: Negotiations Begin
negotiationsBegin.description: 8-month bargaining process starts with mediation included. Discussions focus on wages, working conditions, and ground time pay.

bargainingStalls.title: Bargaining Stalls
bargainingStalls.description: Air Canada offers 38% pay increase over 4 years. Union rejects offer citing insufficient ground time pay and parity demands.

strikeVote.title: Strike Mandate Approved
strikeVote.description: CUPE members vote 99.7% in favor of strike action, giving union strong mandate for work stoppage if negotiations fail.

finalOfferRejected.title: Final Offer Rejected
finalOfferRejected.description: Air Canada tables revised offer but union rejects due to continued lack of adequate ground time compensation.

strikeLockoutNotices.title: Strike & Lockout Notices
strikeLockoutNotices.description: Both parties serve 72-hour notices. Air Canada requests government arbitration while union maintains strike position.

preStrikeCancellations.title: Pre-Strike Disruptions
preStrikeCancellations.description: Over 600 flights cancelled before official strike begins. Express flights continue operating as planned.

strikeBegins.title: Strike Begins
strikeBegins.description: CUPE flight attendants officially walk off job. Air Canada locks out staff and suspends all mainline and Rouge operations.

govIntervention.title: Government Orders Arbitration
govIntervention.description: Jobs Minister Patty Hajdu orders binding arbitration, suspends strike, and mandates immediate return to work.

ongoingIssues.title: Ongoing Developments
ongoingIssues.description: Mass refunds and rebooking begin. Picket lines continue with reports of 'sick out' activities and cease-and-desist letters issued.

dayOfAction.title: Day of Action Protests
dayOfAction.description: Coast-to-coast demonstrations at airports across Canada. Union receives overwhelming public support and solidarity from other labor groups.

cirbOrder.title: CIRB Orders Return to Work
cirbOrder.description: Canada Industrial Relations Board officially orders Air Canada to resume operations and flight attendants to return to duties. Collective agreement extended.

unionDefiance.title: Union Defies CIRB Order
unionDefiance.description: CUPE refuses to return to work, maintains strike until government orders Air Canada back to bargaining table. Strike continues despite legal order.

tentativeDeal.title: Tentative Deal Reached — Strike Ends
tentativeDeal.description: After 9 hours of mediated talks, CUPE and Air Canada reach a tentative agreement. The 3-day strike officially ends. Flights gradually resume August 19.

ratificationVote.title: Ratification Vote: 99.1% Reject Wage Offer
ratificationVote.description: Flight attendants overwhelmingly reject the wage portion of the tentative agreement with 99.1% voting against. Only the wage component is sent to binding arbitration.

arbitratorRuling.title: Arbitrator Issues Final Wage Ruling
arbitratorRuling.description: The arbitrator's final ruling awards junior mainline flight attendants a 12% increase in year one, senior crew 8%, and Rouge attendants 13%. The labour dispute officially concludes.

bindingArbitration.title: Binding Arbitration Process Initiated
bindingArbitration.description: The Canada Industrial Relations Board will oversee fast-tracked arbitration. Both parties must accept the arbitrator's final decision on wages, benefits, and working conditions.

operationalRecovery.title: Gradual Flight Restoration Underway
operationalRecovery.description: Air Canada begins gradual restoration of flight operations. Passengers advised to expect continued disruptions during recovery period with mass refunds and rebooking available.

unionResponse.title: CUPE Condemns Government Intervention
unionResponse.description: Union leadership expresses strong disappointment with forced arbitration, saying right to strike has been removed and collective bargaining undermined.

strikeVoteDetails.title: 99.7% Strike Vote Mandate
strikeVoteDetails.description: CUPE members delivered overwhelming support for strike action with 99.7% approval, demonstrating unprecedented unity among flight attendants on key issues.

ongoingProtests.title: Continued Protest Activities
ongoingProtests.description: Despite government order, picket lines continue with reports of 'sick out' activities. Air Canada issues cease-and-desist letters for alleged illegal strike activities.
